era_context: Early Dynastic Period in Mesopotamia (c. 3150–2334 BCE); Egypt's First Dynasty (c. 3100–2890 BCE). The Indus Valley Civilization is in its early phases. Writing systems are nascent or recently developed in Mesopotamia and Egypt.

events.title: Mesopotamian Early Dynastic I period
events.description: Sumerian city-states continued to consolidate power and develop complex administrative systems. This year falls within the Early Dynastic I phase. No specific events are recorded for 3114 BCE.
events.certainty_note: Dating is based on stratigraphic sequences and pottery typology. Absolute dating in this era is uncertain by ±50–100 years.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed. The year 3114 BCE has no attested specific events.
historiographic_note: The historical record for 3114 BCE is entirely archaeological. No contemporary written records exist. The chronology is based on stratigraphic sequences and pottery typology.
